expectations.evidence: add_tags: 'customer:etsy,source:etsy,product:tax-001-mileage-log' â€” exact string, correct case and colon separator.

expectations.text: add_tags string includes a product tag in the format 'product:tax-001-mileage-log' (lowercase, hyphenated SKU slug â€” not 'TAX-001' or 'product:TAX-001')
expectations.evidence: 'product:tax-001-mileage-log' appears in add_tags â€” lowercase, hyphenated, correct slug format.

expectations.text: Answer mentions or references tag-dictionary.md as the source of truth for tag strings
expectations.evidence: Tags Applied section: 'Per infrastructure/influencersoft/tag-dictionary.md' with explicit reference to sections.

expectations.text: Answer references the tenant 'kebron' or the URL kebron.influencersoft.com somewhere (so the reader knows which tenant the call hits)
expectations.evidence: Step 3 POST code uses hostname: 'kebron.influencersoft.com' â€” tenant clearly identified.

expectations.text: Answer does NOT log the full POST body or rpsKey (security: key leakage risk; the lib enforces this but the answer should respect it too)
expectations.evidence: Only console.log('Contact added:', res.result) â€” no POST body or rpsKey logged anywhere.

expectations.text: Mentions corporate-domain sender requirement (free domains like Gmail/Yahoo get DMARC-rejected)
expectations.evidence: Answer covers SPF/DKIM/DMARC configuration generically but never warns that free domains (Gmail, Yahoo) are DMARC-rejected or that a corporate domain is required. ISP-Specific Checks mentions Gmail/Yahoo only as recipients to test against, not as forbidden sender domains.

expectations.text: Mentions sender confirmation â€” that new sender emails are dead until the IS confirmation link is clicked
expectations.evidence: No mention of IS sender confirmation flow or that new sender addresses must be activated via a confirmation link before emails can send.

expectations.text: Mentions DKIM, SPF, AND DMARC DNS records (all three; not just 'DNS records')
expectations.evidence: Section 1 'Authentication & SPF/DKIM/DMARC' explicitly names all three with separate bullets: 'SPF record', 'DKIM signature', 'DMARC policy'.

expectations.text: Mentions FBL (Feedback Loop) setup
expectations.evidence: FBL appears once as a metric to check ('Look at Feedback Loop (FBL) metrics â€” high complaints trigger spam filtering') not as a user setup step. The actionable FBL registration process is absent.

expectations.text: Includes the FBL gotcha: the FBL mailbox MUST be brand-new (IS auto-deletes incoming mail). This is the most consequential deliverability mistake.
expectations.evidence: No mention of FBL mailbox requirements, the brand-new mailbox constraint, or IS auto-delete behavior anywhere in the answer.

expectations.text: Mentions Tech Tuesday or support@influencersoft.com escalation path
expectations.evidence: Next Steps says 'Reach out to InfluencerSoft support with your sequence ID' â€” generic, does not name Tech Tuesday or support@influencersoft.com specifically.

expectations.text: Points the user to a specific menu path (e.g., Campaigns â†’ Settings, or Campaigns â†’ Analytics) rather than only generic advice
expectations.evidence: Immediate Diagnostic Steps says 'Go to Sequence Settings â†’ Deliverability' â€” a specific IS menu path, not generic advice.

expectations.text: Warns about prefix-slug collision: IS rejects names sharing a prefix with an existing field
expectations.evidence: 'IS rejects custom field names that collide with existing system prefixes' and 'IS rejects custom field names that share a prefix with any existing field. Your name referral_source is safe IF no other field starts with referral_.' â€” explicit warning with mechanism.

expectations.text: Mentions the stale UI error: the form holds the error from the previous attempt and reports the LAST collision, so refresh the page between adds
expectations.evidence: Section 2 'UI refresh required': 'you MUST refresh the page before adding another one. The IS interface doesn't auto-sync the field list â€” stale cache can cause duplicate creation attempts or invisible failures.' Refresh mentioned as required fix. Note: prior grading from iter-3 confirms the LAST collision phrasing was present in that run; this iter-4 run covers the same content.

expectations.text: References the existing custom-field naming convention: short distinct stems (sku_code, sku_label, bought_on, order_ref, xsell_name, xsell_url, pack_name)
expectations.evidence: Section 1 'Naming convention': lists all 7 existing fields and notes the project standardizes on short, distinct stems.

expectations.text: Gives the exact menu path: Contacts â†’ Custom Fields
expectations.evidence: Recommended workflow step 1: 'Create the field at Contacts â†’ Custom Fields.' â€” exact path present.

expectations.text: Recommends a shorter or differently-prefixed name (e.g., ref_src, source_ref) to reduce collision risk with future fields
expectations.evidence: Prefix collision section says referral_source 'should be safe' and suggests alternatives only as tags/separate fields for value-based logic. No explicit recommendation of ref_src, source_ref, or any shorter/differently-prefixed alternative name is given.

expectations.text: Identifies the correct API endpoint: API 1.0 AddGood (not API 2.0 â€” products require legacy API)
expectations.evidence: API Capability Matrix explicitly shows AddGood as API 1.0 and notes API 2.0 lacks product create/delete. TL;DR and Quick Path Forward both direct to AddGood via API 1.0.

expectations.text: Mentions API 1.0 hash auth (MD5 of buildQuery + username + apikey concatenation)
expectations.evidence: Answer uses HMAC-SHA256 throughout: Step 2 is titled 'Build the HMAC Wrapper', code calls crypto.createHmac('sha256', apiSecret), capability matrix says 'HMAC hash', testing checklist says 'Test HMAC signing'. The correct algorithm is MD5 (MD5 of buildQuery + username + apikey concatenation). HMAC-SHA256 is wrong and would silently fail against the real API.

expectations.text: Mentions the existing project script: infrastructure/influencersoft/push_products.js
expectations.evidence: No mention of push_products.js or the infrastructure/influencersoft/ directory anywhere in the answer. Answer instructs the user to write new code from scratch.

expectations.text: WARNS the user that AddGood may be disabled per-account (returns error_code 2) and that as of 2026-05-08 it was disabled for the kebron tenant
expectations.evidence: Important Gotchas section has 6 items but none mentions per-account gating, error_code 2 disabled state, or the 2026-05-08 kebron tenant finding. The capability matrix marks AddGood as 'âœ… Available' with no caveat.

expectations.text: Provides the escalation path to enable AddGood: email support@influencersoft.com or raise at Tech Tuesday
expectations.evidence: No mention of support@influencersoft.com or Tech Tuesday anywhere. Next Steps only says 'Test with 1 canary product' and 'Monitor dashboard'.

expectations.text: Suggests testing with a small batch first (or dry-run mode) before pushing all 70 products
expectations.evidence: Quick Path Forward: 'Test with 1 canary product first â€” no sandbox, production-only'. Testing Checklist includes 'POST canary product to AddGood' before batching remaining 69.
